name: Cherry Berry Pie
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 cups Frozen unsweetened pitted cherries, thawed and drained
1/3 cup Quick cooking tapioca (for cherry layer)
1 1/2 tablespoons Quick cooking tapioca (for cherry layer)
Optional: Chopped almond and flaked coconut
recipeInstructions:
1. For the Crust:
a. Combine flour, salt, and baking powder in a bowl.
b. Cut in shortening and butter flavor shortening with a pastry blender or 2 knives until the mixture forms pea-sized chunks.
c. Gradually combine water and vinegar.
d. Sprinkle dough over flour mixture, about 1 tablespoon at a time.
e. Toss lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide into two portions.
f. Press each portion between your hands to form two 5 to 6-inch pancakes.
g. Flour the rolling surface and roll dough for the bottom crust into a circle.
h. Trim 1 inch larger than an upside-down 9-inch pie plate.
i. Loosen dough carefully, fold it into quarters, and unfold.
j. Press into the pie plate.
k. Trim edge even with the pie plate.
2. For the Cherry Layer:
a. Thaw and drain enough berries to yield 2 to 2 1/2 cups fruit and 1/2 cup juice.
b. Combine cherries, 1/3 cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon tapioca, 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch, almond extract, and vanilla extract in a large bowl.
c. Combine 1/2 cup cherry juice, 1 tablespoon granulated sugar, 1 1/2 tablespoons tapioca, and 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch in a small saucepan.
d. Place on medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, for 3 or 4 minutes.
e. Cool slightly and pour over cherries.
f. Mix gently until the cherries are coated.
3. For the Cream Cheese Layer:
a. Combine cream cheese, confectioners sugar, chopped almonds, almond extract, and vanilla extract in a medium bowl.
b. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended.
c. Spread over the bottom of the unbaked pie shell.
4. For the Raspberry Layer:
a. Combine raspberries, granulated sugar, and cornstarch in a bowl.
b. Mix carefully.
c. Spoon over cream cheese layer.
d. If desired, place raspberry mixture in a saucepan and cook briefly until thickened before spooning over cream cheese layer.
5. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
a. Roll the top crust into a circle, cut into 10 - 1/2-inch strips.
b. Place 5 strips evenly across the filling.
c. Fold every other strip back.
d. Lay first strip across in the opposite direction.
e. Continue in this pattern, folding back every other strip each time you add a cross strip.
f. Trim ends of lattice strips even with the crust.
g. Fold edge under and flute.
6. Brush pastry with egg white and sprinkle lightly with granulated sugar.
a. Bake at 400 degrees F for 15 minutes.
b. Reduce temperature to 350 degrees F and bake an additional 40 minutes.
c. Sprinkle with chopped almonds and flaked coconut, if desired, 5 minutes before the end of baking time.
d. Cool until barely warm or to room temperature before serving.
Note: If using fresh cherries, use 3 to 4 cups in the pie. Mash and press additional cherries through a sieve or colander to obtain 1/2 cup of juice.
